name: LitmusChaos
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); ChaosCenter SaaS (Harness Chaos Engineering) has commercial tier

auth.notes: ChaosCenter API key; Kubernetes RBAC for in-cluster execution; GitHub/Google OAuth for ChaosCenter UI

performance.notes: REST API ~200ms; chaos experiment execution is async (minutes)

agent_readiness.notes: CNCF-hosted Kubernetes-native chaos engineering platform. ChaosHub with 100+ experiments. ChaosCenter web UI + REST API. Chaos workflows for complex multi-step experiments. Probes for automated verification (HTTP, command, k8s resource). Resilience scoring. Integrates with CI/CD. Graduated CNCF project.

gotchas: Kubernetes-native — limited utility outside k8s environments
ChaosCenter web UI can be complex to navigate for beginners
REST API documentation less complete than UI-focused docs
Requires cluster-admin permissions for most experiments
best_when: Kubernetes-native chaos engineering with large experiment library
CI/CD pipelines with chaos resilience scoring gates
Platform engineering teams standardizing chaos experiments across services
CNCF-aligned organizations wanting graduated open-source chaos platform
avoid_when: Teams wanting easier UX (Gremlin more user-friendly)
Quick ad-hoc chaos experiments (simpler tools work)
summary: LitmusChaos is a CNCF-graduated Kubernetes-native chaos engineering platform. ChaosHub with 100+ experiments. ChaosCenter web UI and REST API. Chaos workflows for complex experiments with automated probes. Resilience scoring for CI/CD gates. Best for Kubernetes-native teams wanting a comprehensive open-source chaos engineering platform with a large experiment library.
